name: Homemade Yogurt
recipeIngredient: skimmed milk may be used -- or
recipeInstructions:
I make this all of the time and it is wonderful. One may add a sweetener
and/or fruit after it comes off the heating pad.
Turn a heating pad to low. Sterilize 4 quart Mason jars along with lids
and rims.
Place the milk in a large stainless-steel pot and gently heat to 180°F (82°C).
Remove from the heat and cool to 115°F (46°C). Remove 1 cup of the milk
from the pan and stir in 1 tablespoon of the yogurt starter (see note;
I use 16 ounces plain yogurt and always set aside this amount for my next
batch; with the next batch, use 3.5 quarts milk with the 16 ounces of
yogurt). Either way, stir in the yogurt starter/milk mixture or the
reserved yogurt into the pot. Stir well so that the yogurt is smooth.
Fill 4 sterilized quart jars and seal with sterilized lids and rims.
Place the four quart jars on the heating pad. Cover with a large towel so
that the jars stay warm. Keep on the heating pad for 8-16 hours (the longer,
the tangier the yogurt). Refrigerate.
